page: Microsoft Azure NC36lds_xl_RTXPRO6000BSE_v6 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine belonging to the NCdsRTXPRO6000BSEv6XL family. It is powered by an Intel processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 36 dedicated vCPUs. The system is provisioned with 72.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration features a single GPU and includes 1980 GB of local storage. It is also capable of utilizing Premium Storage. A key architectural tradeoff is the low system memory density relative to the high vCPU count, which restricts its suitability for memory-intensive workloads. Additionally, the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the combination of a dedicated GPU, 36 vCPUs, and substantial local storage, this instance is designed for GPU-accelerated compute tasks, graphics rendering, and machine learning workloads that do not require massive system memory footprints. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the low memory-to-core ratio positions this server as a specialized option where budget is optimized toward GPU and compute resources rather than high-capacity RAM.
description: Microsoft Azure NC36lds_xl_RTXPRO6000BSE_v6 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine from the NCdsRTXPRO6000BSEv6XL family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 36 dedicated Intel vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, paired with 72.0 GB of system memory, which represents a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The hardware configuration includes a single GPU and 1980 GB of local storage, with support for Premium Storage. This server does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Due to the low memory density, it is best suited for GPU-accelerated compute tasks, graphics rendering, and specialized workloads that prioritize parallel processing and local storage capacity over large system memory allocations.
